skill.name: ensembl-database
skill.description: Access Ensembl REST API for vertebrate genomic data; use when you need gene/ID lookups, sequence retrieval, variant effect prediction (VEP), or homology/assembly coordinate mapping.
skill.summary: Query the public Ensembl REST API for vertebrate genomic data including gene lookups, sequence retrieval, and variant effect prediction.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill connects to the public Ensembl REST API (rest.ensembl.org) for legitimate genomic data queries. All 68 static analysis findings were evaluated: 66 are false positives triggered by markdown formatting and non-cryptographic content, and 2 are benign network/HTTP library usage inherent to the skill's documented purpose. No malicious intent, prompt injection, or data exfiltration patterns were detected.

medium_findings.title: External script execution via Python subprocess
medium_findings.description: The skill invokes scripts/query_ensembl.py through documented shell commands. This is expected core functionality for the skill, but users should be aware that arbitrary Python code is executed within the skill's directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Shell commands are documented in markdown code blocks for the skill's Python script. The commands reference the packaged scripts/query_ensembl.py file which exists and has been verified.

medium_findings.title: Network requests to external API
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP GET requests to https://rest.ensembl.org for gene lookups, sequence retrieval, and VEP. This is the documented purpose of the skill, but requires network egress.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of requests.get() calls to rest.ensembl.org in query_ensembl.py. The target is the legitimate public Ensembl API from EMBL-EBI, a well-known bioinformatics resource.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer reported 'weak cryptographic algorithm' across JSON score file, Python script, and markdown. No actual cryptographic operations exist. Scanner likely triggered on file extensions (.md) or text patterns in non-code contexts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed no cryptographic code exists at any of these locations. JSON file contains score data, Python line 7 is argparse setup, and SKILL.md contains markdown description text.

low_findings.title: Ruby/shell backtick execution (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick-enclosed text as Ruby shell execution. These are standard markdown code spans used for formatting API endpoint paths, parameter names, and code examples. No shell execution or Ruby code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All flagged locations contain markdown code spans describing REST API endpoint paths (e.g., GET /lookup/symbol/{species}/{symbol}), not executable shell commands.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged error messages and markdown documentation text as system reconnaissance. Items include standard Python stderr error output and descriptive text in skill documentation. No reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Flagged lines contain: JSON audit note about testing scope, Python stderr error message for missing --id parameter, and markdown documentation text describing when to use the skill.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL to public API (benign)
low_findings.description: All flagged URLs reference the public Ensembl REST API (https://rest.ensembl.org) or the source GitHub repository. These are legitimate references to the data source the skill is designed to query.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs point to rest.ensembl.org (EMBL-EBI public bioinformatics API) or the skill's GitHub repository. These are the legitimate and documented data sources for this skill.

content.user_title: Query the Ensembl Database for Genomic Data
content.value_statement: Researchers need quick access to vertebrate genomic data from the Ensembl database. This skill automates gene lookups, sequence retrieval, and variant effect prediction through the Ensembl REST API.
actual_capabilities: Look up gene symbols and Ensembl IDs to retrieve genomic annotations and metadata
Retrieve DNA, cDNA, and protein sequences from Ensembl by stable identifier
Predict functional consequences of genetic variants using the Variant Effect Predictor (VEP)
Query the Ensembl REST API for structured, reproducible results
Run scripted workflows through the command-line interface for repeatable data retrieval
limitations: Requires network access to the Ensembl REST API at rest.ensembl.org
Rate limited to 15 requests per second by the Ensembl API service
Supports only gene lookup, sequence retrieval, and VEP actions from the full Ensembl API
Requires Python 3.8+ and the requests library for fallback HTTP queries

use_cases.title: Gene Annotation Lookup
use_cases.description: Resolve gene symbols like BRCA2 to Ensembl identifiers and retrieve structured gene metadata for research publications.

use_cases.title: Variant Consequence Analysis
use_cases.description: Submit HGVS variant notations to the Ensembl VEP endpoint and receive functional consequence predictions for clinical interpretation.

use_cases.title: Genomic Sequence Extraction
use_cases.description: Retrieve DNA or protein sequences by Ensembl stable ID in JSON format for downstream analysis pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Look Up a Gene by Symbol
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the gene {symbol} in species {species} using the Ensembl database.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs basic gene information from a known symbol.

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve a Genomic Sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the sequence for Ensembl ID {id}. Use the sequence action.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher needs the DNA sequence for a known Ensembl transcript.

prompt_templates.title: Predict Variant Consequences
prompt_templates.prompt: Run VEP analysis on the variant {hgvs} in {species} to determine its functional impact.
prompt_templates.scenario: Clinician needs to know the functional effects of a specific genetic variant.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Species Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the orthologs of gene {symbol} in {species} across all available vertebrate species in Ensembl.
prompt_templates.scenario: Evolutionary biologist needs cross-species gene comparisons.

output_examples.input: Look up the gene BRCA2 in human
output: Description: breast cancer 2, DNA repair associated

output_examples.input: Predict the effect of variant ENST00000380152.8:c.68_69delAG in human

best_practices: Always specify both --species and --symbol when performing gene lookups to ensure accurate results.
Use the Ensembl stable ID format (ENSG, ENST, ENSP) for sequence retrieval for the most reliable matching.
Verify network connectivity to https://rest.ensembl.org before running batch queries to avoid timeout errors.
anti_patterns: Do not assume HGVS notation formats that do not match Ensembl transcript IDs will produce valid VEP results.
Do not run high-volume queries without implementing rate limiting or retry logic for HTTP 429 responses.
Do not use the lookup action without specifying a species, as this will cause the script to exit with an error.

faq.question: What data does this skill access?
faq.answer: This skill queries the public Ensembl REST API to retrieve vertebrate genomic data including gene annotations, sequences, and variant effect predictions.

faq.question: Do I need an API key?
faq.answer: No. The Ensembl REST API is publicly accessible without authentication. However, network access to https://rest.ensembl.org is required.

faq.question: What is the rate limit for Ensembl API queries?
faq.answer: The Ensembl REST API allows up to 15 requests per second. The skill handles HTTP 429 responses with automatic retry.

faq.question: Which species are supported?
faq.answer: This skill supports all vertebrate species available in Ensembl, including human, mouse, zebrafish, and many others.

faq.question: Can I use this skill offline?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires network access to https://rest.ensembl.org to query genomic data.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: Results are returned in JSON format from the Ensembl REST API, formatted with indentation for readability.
